   135→
   136→== Overview
   137→
   138→The
   139→{jjdgr_gallops}
   140→is the persistent data store for Job Jockey.
   141→It tracks
   142→{jjdhr_heat_s}
   143→(bounded initiatives) and their constituent
   144→{jjdpr_pace_s}
   145→(discrete actions).
   146→Each
   147→{jjdpr_pace}
   148→maintains a history of
   149→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   150→(plan refinements).
   151→
   152→== Design Principles
   153→
   154→=== Exclusive JSON Ownership
   155→
   156→All access to the
   157→{jjdgr_gallops}
   158→goes through {jjdx_cli}, the Job Jockey subcommand of the Voce Viva executor.
   159→No jq or direct JSON manipulation ever touches this data.
   160→This is an absolute architectural constraint, not a preference.
   161→
   162→The rationale: writing reliable bash with jq pipelines requires fighting LLM training data.
   163→The internet is full of bad bash.
   164→Rust replaces fragile jq invocations with compiler-enforced correctness.
   165→The compiler is tireless; humans are not.
   166→
   167→=== Git as Journal
   168→
   169→The
   170→{jjdgr_gallops}
   171→remains JSON specifically so git can serve as the journalling layer.
   172→Every mutation creates a diffable commit.
   173→Git owns the complete history; we need not implement our own audit trail.
   174→
   175→The
   176→{jjdkr_tack}
   177→history within each
   178→{jjdpr_pace}
   179→exists for convenient postmortem analysis.
   180→Git has the full history, but
   181→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   182→let us see plan evolution without spelunking commits.
   183→
   184→=== Diff-Friendly Mutations
   185→
   186→New
   187→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   188→prepend to position 0 rather than append.
   189→This produces cleaner git diffs: prepending adds a line with trailing comma, while appending would modify the previous last line to add a comma.
   190→Small discipline, large cumulative benefit.
   191→
   192→The
   193→{jjdhm_order}
   194→array contains full
   195→{jjdt_coronet_s}.
   196→Each
   197→{jjdt_coronet}
   198→embeds its parent
   199→{jjdt_firemark}
   200→(redundant within a single
   201→{jjdhr_heat}).
   202→This redundancy is cheap and enables grep, copy-paste, and self-documenting JSON.
   203→
   204→=== Crash-Safe Architecture
   205→
   206→Bash handles locking via `git update-ref`.
   207→{jjdx_cli} owns all
   208→{jjdgr_gallops}
   209→file access - reading, transforming, and writing.
   210→{jjdx_cli} implements atomic writes internally (write to temp file, then rename).
   211→If {jjdx_cli} crashes mid-transform, no partial writes occur.
   212→
   213→The pattern:
   214→[source,bash]
   215→----
   216→lock_acquire
   217→vvx jjx gallops nominate --file gallops.json "my-heat" "260111" || { lock_release; die; }
   218→lock_release
   219→----
   220→
   221→This keeps bash simple (just locking and invocation) while {jjdx_cli} owns all JSON file operations.
   222→
   223→=== Deterministic Serialization
   224→
   225→Implementations MUST serialize JSON object keys in deterministic order to minimize diff churn.
   226→For `heats` and `paces` objects, use sorted key order (e.g., Rust `BTreeMap` instead of `HashMap`).
   227→This ensures that reordering paces via
   228→{jjdo_rail}
   229→produces minimal diffs — only the `order` array changes, not the entire `paces` object.
   230→
   231→=== Identity Encoding
   232→
   233→{jjdt_firemark}
   234→(`₣` prefix) and
   235→{jjdt_coronet}
   236→(`₢` prefix) are the canonical identities used in JSON keys, CLI arguments, and display.
   237→The base64 encoding provides compact, URL-safe, shell-safe strings.
   238→Internally, Rust may decode to integers for arithmetic, but the string form is authoritative.
   239→
   240→=== Horse Racing Vocabulary
   241→
   242→Job Jockey uses horse racing metaphors throughout.
   243→This is intentional: vivid domain vocabulary helps humans hold complex systems in working memory.
   244→
   245→* {jjdhr_heat} - a bounded initiative (like a race)
   246→* {jjdpr_pace} - a discrete action (the rhythm of the race)
   247→* {jjdkr_tack} - plan refinement (equipment; also "tacking" = adjusting course)
   248→* {jjdt_firemark} - heat identity, `₣`-prefixed (hot-iron brand marking ownership)
   249→* {jjdt_coronet} - pace identity, `₢`-prefixed (marking at the hoof line)
   250→* {jjdt_silks} - display name (jockey's colors)
   251→* {jjdgr_gallops} - the training grounds (where real work happens before race day)
   252→
   253→== Types
   254→
   255→[[jjdt_firemark]]
   256→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   257→{jjdt_firemark}::
   258→The identity of a
   259→{jjdhr_heat}.
   260→Format: `₣` + 2 base64 characters (e.g., `₣AB`).
   261→The 2 base64 characters encode an integer 0-4095 (2^12).
   262→Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
   263→Input may omit the `₣` prefix; output always includes it.
   264→
   265→[[jjdt_coronet]]
   266→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   267→{jjdt_coronet}::
   268→The identity of a
   269→{jjdpr_pace}.
   270→Format: `₢` + 5 base64 characters (e.g., `₢ABCDE`).
   271→Globally unique: the first 2 base64 characters are the parent
   272→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   273→encoded identity; the remaining 3 encode the pace index (0-262143, 2^18).
   274→Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
   275→Input may omit the `₢` prefix; output always includes it.
   276→+
   277→*Reassignment:* When a
   278→{jjdpr_pace}
   279→is moved between
   280→{jjdhr_heat_s}
   281→via
   282→{jjdo_draft},
   283→the old
   284→{jjdt_coronet}
   285→becomes invalid and a new one is assigned using the destination
   286→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   287→{jjdhm_seed}.
   288→The new
   289→{jjdt_coronet}
   290→embeds the destination
   291→{jjdt_firemark}.
   292→{jjdkr_tack}
   293→history is preserved, with a note recording the original
   294→{jjdt_coronet}.
   295→
   296→[[jjdt_silks]]
   297→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   298→{jjdt_silks}::
   299→A kebab-case display name.
   300→Used for human-readable identification of
   301→{jjdhr_heat_s}
   302→and
   303→{jjdpr_pace_s}.
   304→Pattern: `[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*`
   305→
   306→== Records
   307→
   308→=== {jjdgr_gallops}
   309→
   310→[[jjdgr_gallops]]
   311→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   312→{jjdgr_gallops}::
   313→The root JSON structure containing all Job Jockey data.
   314→Singleton record persisted at `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.
   315→
   316→==== Members
   317→
   318→* [[jjdgm_seed]]
   319→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   320→{jjdgm_seed}
   321→is the next
   322→{jjdt_firemark}
   323→to allocate, stored as 2 base64 characters.
   324→Incremented by
   325→{jjdo_nominate}.
   326→
   327→=== {jjdhr_heat}
   328→
   329→[[jjdhr_heat]]
   330→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   331→{jjdhr_heat}::
   332→A bounded initiative with coherent goals.
   333→Contains zero or more
   334→{jjdpr_pace_s}
   335→representing discrete actions.
   336→Keyed in JSON by
   337→{jjdt_firemark}.
   338→
   339→==== Members
   340→
   341→* [[jjdhm_firemark]]
   342→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   343→{jjdhm_firemark}
   344→is the
   345→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   346→primary key.
   347→Type:
   348→{jjdt_firemark}.
   349→Implicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.
   350→
   351→* [[jjdhm_silks]]
   352→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   353→{jjdhm_silks}
   354→is the
   355→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   356→display name.
   357→Type:
   358→{jjdt_silks}.
   359→
   360→* [[jjdhm_created]]
   361→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   362→{jjdhm_created}
   363→records when the
   364→{jjdhr_heat}
   365→was nominated.
   366→Format: YYMMDD.
   367→
   368→* [[jjdhm_status]]
   369→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   370→{jjdhm_status}
   371→indicates the
   372→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   373→lifecycle state.
   374→Values:
   375→{jjdhe_racing},
   376→{jjdhe_stabled},
   377→or
   378→{jjdhe_retired}.
   379→
   380→* [[jjdhm_order]]
   381→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_repeated⟧
   382→{jjdhm_order}
   383→lists
   384→{jjdt_coronet_s}
   385→in execution sequence.
   386→May be reordered via
   387→{jjdo_rail}.
   388→
   389→* [[jjdhm_seed]]
   390→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   391→{jjdhm_seed}
   392→is the next
   393→{jjdt_coronet}
   394→index to allocate, stored as 3 base64 characters.
   395→Incremented by
   396→{jjdo_slate}.
   397→
   398→* [[jjdhm_paddock]]
   399→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   400→{jjdhm_paddock}
   401→is the path to the paddock file containing
   402→{jjdhr_heat}
   403→context and background.
   404→Format: `.claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md` where XX is the base64-encoded
   405→{jjdt_firemark}
   406→(without `₣` prefix).
   407→Created by
   408→{jjdo_nominate}.
   409→
   410→==== Status Values
   411→
   412→[[jjdhe_racing]]
   413→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   414→{jjdhe_racing}::
   415→The
   416→{jjdhr_heat}
   417→is actively being worked.
   418→
   419→[[jjdhe_stabled]]
   420→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   421→{jjdhe_stabled}::
   422→The
   423→{jjdhr_heat}
   424→is paused, not actively worked.
   425→
   426→[[jjdhe_retired]]
   427→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   428→{jjdhe_retired}::
   429→The
   430→{jjdhr_heat}
   431→is complete and archived.
   432→
   433→=== {jjdpr_pace}
   434→
   435→[[jjdpr_pace]]
   436→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   437→{jjdpr_pace}::
   438→A discrete action within a
   439→{jjdhr_heat}.
   440→Contains one or more
   441→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   442→representing state snapshots and plan refinements.
   443→Current state is derived from `tacks[0].state`.
   444→Current silks is derived from `tacks[0].silks`.
   445→Keyed in JSON by
   446→{jjdt_coronet}.
   447→
   448→==== Members
   449→
   450→* [[jjdpm_coronet]]
   451→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   452→{jjdpm_coronet}
   453→is the
   454→{jjdpr_pace_p}
   455→globally unique identity.
   456→Type:
   457→{jjdt_coronet}.
   458→Implicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.
   459→
   460→=== {jjdkr_tack}
   461→
   462→[[jjdkr_tack]]
   463→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   464→{jjdkr_tack}::
   465→A snapshot of
   466→{jjdpr_pace}
   467→state and plan at a point in time.
   468→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   469→form an append-only history capturing both plan refinements and state transitions.
   470→Position 0 is the most recent; older
   471→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   472→follow.
   473→New
   474→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   475→are prepended for cleaner git diffs.
   476→The current
   477→{jjdpr_pace}
   478→state is always
   479→`tacks[0].state`.
   480→
   481→==== Members
   482→
   483→* [[jjdkm_refined]]
   484→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   485→{jjdkm_refined}
   486→records when this
   487→{jjdkr_tack}
   488→was added.
   489→Format: YYMMDD-HHMM.
   490→
   491→* [[jjdkm_state]]
   492→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   493→{jjdkm_state}
   494→indicates the
   495→{jjdpr_pace_p}
   496→progress at this snapshot.
   497→Values:
   498→{jjdpe_rough},
   499→{jjdpe_bridled},
   500→{jjdpe_complete},
   501→or
   502→{jjdpe_abandoned}.
   503→
   504→* [[jjdkm_text]]
   505→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   506→{jjdkm_text}
   507→contains the plan specification.
   508→Non-empty string describing what the
   509→{jjdpr_pace}
   510→should accomplish.
   511→
   512→* [[jjdkm_silks]]
   513→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   514→{jjdkm_silks}
   515→is the
   516→{jjdpr_pace_p}
   517→display name at this point in time.
   518→Type:
   519→{jjdt_silks}.
   520→Enables rename history; current pace silks is derived from `tacks[0].silks`.
   521→
   522→* [[jjdkm_commit]]
   523→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   524→{jjdkm_commit}
   525→is the commit SHA at
   526→{jjdkr_tack}
   527→creation.
   528→Format: 7-character hex string.
   529→Captures repo state when plan was written.
   530→Value `0000000` indicates commit was unavailable at creation time.
   531→
   532→* [[jjdkm_direction]]
   533→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_conditional⟧
   534→{jjdkm_direction}
   535→contains execution guidance for autonomous operation.
   536→Required when
   537→{jjdkm_state}
   538→is
   539→{jjdpe_bridled};
   540→forbidden otherwise.
   541→Describes which agent to dispatch, parallelism strategy, or other execution parameters.
   542→
   543→==== State Values
   544→
   545→[[jjdpe_rough]]
   546→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   547→{jjdpe_rough}::
   548→The
   549→{jjdpr_pace}
   550→needs clarification before work can proceed.
   551→When context is assembled for a
   552→{jjdpe_rough}
   553→pace, the model is guided to refine the specification rather than begin execution.
   554→
   555→[[jjdpe_bridled]]
   556→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   557→{jjdpe_bridled}::
   558→The
   559→{jjdpr_pace}
   560→is ready for autonomous execution.
   561→The
   562→{jjdkm_direction}
   563→field contains explicit guidance for the executing agent.
   564→When context is assembled for a
   565→{jjdpe_bridled}
   566→pace, the model proceeds without human confirmation.
   567→
   568→[[jjdpe_complete]]
   569→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧

   582→==== State Classification Predicates
   583→
   584→Predicates for querying pace states by semantic category.
   585→
   586→[[jjdpe_defined]] {jjdpe_defined}:: Pace contributes to heat's scope.
   587→* rough: true
   588→* bridled: true
   589→* complete: true
   590→* abandoned: false
   591→

   593→* rough: false

   599→
   600→== Serialization
   601→
   602→[[jjdz_charset]]
   603→{jjdz_charset}::
   604→The 64-character alphabet for identity encoding.
   605→Uses URL-safe base64 (RFC 4648 §5):
   606→`ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789-_`
   607→Position in charset determines numeric value (A=0, B=1, ..., `-`=62, `_`=63).
   608→This alphabet is safe for JSON keys, URLs, shell, git refs, and filenames.
   609→Matches Rust `base64::engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD`.
   610→
   611→[[jjdz_encoding]]
   612→{jjdz_encoding}::
   613→The algorithm for encoding
   614→{jjdt_firemark}
   615→and
   616→{jjdt_coronet}
   617→values.
   618→+
   619→*{jjdt_firemark} (Heat identity):*
   620→+
   621→. Format: `₣` + 2 base64 characters
   622→. Given integer H (0-4095): encode as `₣` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]`
   623→. To decode `₣XY`: H = pos(X) * 64 + pos(Y)
   624→+
   625→*{jjdt_coronet} (Pace identity):*
   626→+
   627→. Format: `₢` + 5 base64 characters
   628→. First 2 characters encode the parent Heat (same as {jjdt_firemark} without prefix)
   629→. Last 3 characters encode the pace index P (0-262143)
   630→. Given Heat H and pace index P: encode as `₢` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]` + `charset[P/4096]` + `charset[(P/64)%64]` + `charset[P%64]`
   631→. To decode `₢XYZAB`: H from XY, P = pos(Z) * 4096 + pos(A) * 64 + pos(B)
   632→+
   633→*Input flexibility:*
   634→+
   635→CLI commands accept identities with or without prefix.
   636→Length determines type: 2 base64 chars = {jjdt_firemark}, 5 base64 chars = {jjdt_coronet}.
   637→Output always includes the prefix (`₣` or `₢`).

   832→
   833→[[jjdx_cli]]
   834→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_command_group⟧
   835→{jjdx_cli}::
   836→The Job Jockey command group within
   837→{jjdx_vvx}.
   838→Invoked as `vvx jjx`.

   842→
   843→== Commit Message Architecture
   844→
   845→All JJ commits include an action code that serves multiple purposes:
   846→- **Parsing steeplechase history**: Distinguishes between different operation types
   847→- **Semantic clarity**: Indicates the nature and context of each commit
   848→- **Consistency**: Applies uniformly across pace-level and heat-level operations
   849→
   850→== Routines
   851→
   852→Routines are internal reusable procedures invoked by operations.
   853→They encapsulate common logic for consistent behavior across the codebase.
   854→
   855→[[jjdr_load]]
   856→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine⟧
   857→=== {jjdr_load}
   858→
   859→Load and validate the
   860→{jjdgr_gallops}
   861→file.
   862→Every operation that reads
   863→{jjdgr_gallops}
   864→invokes this routine.
   865→Returns a validated
   866→{jjdgr_gallops}
   867→only after all checks pass.
   868→
   869→{jjds_arguments}
   870→
   871→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   872→* {jjda_file}
   873→
   874→{jjds_behavior}
   875→
   876→. Read file contents as raw bytes
   877→. Deserialize bytes into
   878→{jjdgr_gallops}
   879→structure
   880→. Reserialize
   881→{jjdgr_gallops}
   882→structure to JSON string
   883→. Compare reserialized bytes against original bytes
   884→. If different:
   885→{axc_fatal}
   886→with diagnostic showing first difference location
   887→. Validate semantic constraints (timestamps, kebab-case silks, invariants)
   888→. If validation fails:
   889→{axc_fatal}
   890→with validation errors
   891→. Return validated
   892→{jjdgr_gallops}
   893→
   894→{jjds_stdout} None (returns structure to caller)
   895→
   896→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero on validation failure or I/O error.
   897→
   898→*Architectural constraint:*
   899→{jjdr_load}
   900→is the ONLY way to obtain a
   901→{jjdgr_gallops}
   902→from disk.
   903→This makes validation bypass architecturally impossible.
   904→The round-trip check (steps 3-5) ensures the file is in canonical form.
   905→The semantic validation (steps 6-7) ensures business rules are satisfied.
   906→
   907→[[jjdr_save]]
   908→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine⟧
   909→=== {jjdr_save}
   910→
   911→Persist
   912→{jjdgr_gallops}
   913→to disk with validation.
   914→Every operation that writes
   915→{jjdgr_gallops}
   916→invokes this routine.
   917→Validates output by loading it back before finalizing.
   918→
   919→{jjds_arguments}
   920→
   921→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   922→*
   923→{jjdgr_gallops}
   924→(in-memory structure to persist)
   925→
   926→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   927→* {jjda_file}
   928→
   929→{jjds_behavior}
   930→
   931→. Serialize
   932→{jjdgr_gallops}
   933→to canonical JSON string
   934→. Write JSON to temporary file (atomic write preparation)
   935→. Call
   936→{jjdr_load}
   937→on temporary file to validate what was written
   938→. If
   939→{jjdr_load}
   940→fails: delete temporary file,
   941→{axc_fatal}
   942→with error
   943→. Rename temporary file to final path (atomic commit)
   944→
   945→{jjds_stdout} None
   946→
   947→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero on validation failure or I/O error.
   948→
   949→*Design rationale:*
   950→{jjdr_save}
   951→reuses
   952→{jjdr_load}
   953→for validation, ensuring a single validation code path for both reading and writing.
   954→If an operation produces invalid output, the save fails before corrupting the file.
   955→
   956→[[jjdr_persist]]
   957→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine⟧
   958→=== {jjdr_persist}
   959→
   960→Persist
   961→{jjdgr_gallops}
   962→state and commit changes to git with a JJ-aware commit message.
   963→This routine combines
   964→{jjdr_save}
   965→with git commit operations to maintain the journalling discipline.
   966→Used by most write operations to ensure every
   967→{jjdgr_gallops}
   968→mutation is tracked in git history.
   969→
   970→{jjds_arguments}
   971→
   972→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   973→* lock (lock handle) — acquired lock from `vvg_lock_acquire`
   974→
   975→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   976→*
   977→{jjdgr_gallops}
   978→(in-memory structure to persist)
   979→
   980→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   981→* {jjda_file}
   982→
   983→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   984→* identity —
   985→{jjdt_firemark}
   986→for heat-level operations or
   987→{jjdt_coronet}
   988→for pace-level operations
   989→
   990→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   991→* message — commit message subject (without JJ prefix or Co-Authored-By)
   992→
   993→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   994→* size_limit — maximum commit size in bytes for size guard check
   995→
   996→{jjds_behavior}
   997→
   998→.
   999→{jjdr_save}
  1000→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1001→to
  1002→{jjda_file}
  1003→. Stage gallops file with `git add {file}`
  1004→. If paddock file exists for this identity, stage it with `git add {paddock_file}`
  1005→. Run size guard check with provided size_limit
  1006→. Determine action code:
  1007→.. If identity is
  1008→{jjdt_firemark}:
  1009→extract from message prefix (`N:`, `S:`, `r:`, `T:`, `D:`, `R:`)
  1010→.. If identity is
  1011→{jjdt_coronet}:
  1012→extract from message prefix (`n:`, `A:`, `W:`, `F:`, `d:`)
  1013→. Format full commit message: `jjb:HALLMARK:IDENTITY:ACTION: message\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>`
  1014→. Run `git commit -m "..."`
  1015→. Return commit hash
  1016→
  1017→{jjds_stdout} Commit hash (returned to caller)
  1018→
  1019→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero on error.
  1020→
  1021→*Commands that use
  1022→{jjdr_persist}:*
  1023→
  1024→*
  1025→{jjdo_nominate}
  1026→— creates new heat with action `N`
  1027→*
  1028→{jjdo_slate}
  1029→— adds new pace with action `S`
  1030→*
  1031→{jjdo_rail}
  1032→— reorders paces with action `r`
  1033→*
  1034→{jjdo_tally}
  1035→— adds new tack with action `T`
  1036→*
  1037→{jjdo_draft}
  1038→— moves pace between heats with action `D`
  1039→*
  1040→{jjdo_furlough}
  1041→— changes heat status (action varies)
  1042→
  1043→*Commands that do NOT use
  1044→{jjdr_persist}:*
  1045→
  1046→*
  1047→{jjdo_retire}
  1048→— uses custom commit logic for trophy archival (stages trophy file, gallops, and paddock deletion; uses 200KB limit)
  1049→*
  1050→{jjdo_chalk}
  1051→— creates empty commits only, no gallops changes
  1052→*
  1053→{jjdo_notch}
  1054→— commits work artifacts, not gallops state
  1055→
  1056→*Design rationale:*
  1057→{jjdr_persist}
  1058→ensures consistent commit message formatting and git discipline across all
  1059→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1060→mutations.
  1061→By centralizing the commit logic, we guarantee that every state change is properly journalled with parseable commit messages for
  1062→{jjdo_rein}
  1063→to reconstruct steeplechase history.
  1064→
  1065→[[jjdr_wrap]]
  1066→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine⟧
  1067→=== {jjdr_wrap}
  1068→
  1069→Mark a pace complete and commit all changes in one self-contained operation.
  1070→This routine combines staging, committing, state transition, and chalk marker creation.
  1071→Used by `/jjc-pace-wrap` to provide a single-command wrap experience.
  1072→
  1073→{jjds_arguments}
  1074→
  1075→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1076→* {jjdt_coronet}
  1077→(positional) — pace to wrap
  1078→
  1079→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1080→* `--size-limit` — maximum commit size in bytes (default: 50KB)
  1081→
  1082→{jjds_behavior}
  1083→
  1084→. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
  1085→. Stage all changes (`git add -A`)
  1086→. Size guard check:
  1087→.. Get staged content size
  1088→.. If over limit: release lock, exit 2
  1089→. Generate commit message via Claude from diff
  1090→. Commit staged changes
  1091→. Transition pace state to complete via
  1092→{jjdo_tally}
  1093→. Create W chalk marker with outcome summary
  1094→. Release lock
  1095→. Return commit hash
  1096→
  1097→{jjds_exit_enumerated}
  1098→
  1099→* 0 — success
  1100→* 1 — general error (parse failure, gallops error, git error)
  1101→* 2 — size guard exceeded (use --size-limit to override)
  1102→
  1103→*Design rationale:*
  1104→{jjdr_wrap}
  1105→consolidates the multi-step wrap workflow into a single vvx command.
  1106→This eliminates the need for slash commands to orchestrate separate notch, tally, and chalk operations.
  1107→The distinct exit code 2 for size guard allows callers to advise users about the --size-limit flag.
  1108→
  1109→== Operations
  1110→
  1111→Operations are implemented as {jjdx_cli} subcommands.
  1112→{jjdx_cli} owns all
  1113→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1114→file access through
  1115→{jjdr_load}
  1116→and
  1117→{jjdr_save}.
  1118→Bash handles locking via `git update-ref` and invokes {jjdx_cli} with
  1119→{jjda_file}.
  1120→
  1121→=== Write Operations
  1122→
  1123→[[jjdo_nominate]]
  1124→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1125→==== {jjdo_nominate}
  1126→
  1127→Create a new
  1128→{jjdhr_heat}
  1129→with empty
  1130→{jjdpr_pace}
  1131→structure.
  1132→
  1133→{jjds_arguments}
  1134→
  1135→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1136→* {jjda_file}
  1137→
  1138→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1139→* {jjda_silks}
  1140→
  1141→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1142→* {jjda_created}
  1143→
  1144→{jjds_stdout} New
  1145→{jjdt_firemark}
  1146→
  1147→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1148→
  1149→{jjds_behavior}
  1150→
  1151→. If
  1152→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1153→file exists:
  1154→{jjdr_load}
  1155→{jjda_file};
  1156→on failure, exit immediately with
  1157→{jjdr_load}
  1158→error status
  1159→. If
  1160→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1161→file does not exist:
  1162→.. Create parent directory `.claude/jjm/` if needed
  1163→.. Initialize new
  1164→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1165→with
  1166→{jjdgm_seed}
  1167→`AA` and empty `heats`
  1168→. Read current
  1169→{jjdgm_seed}
  1170→as
  1171→{jjdt_firemark}
  1172→. Compute paddock path: `.claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md` where XX is the 2 base64 chars
  1173→. Create paddock file with template:
  1174→+
  1175→----
  1176→# Paddock: {silks}
  1177→
  1178→## Context
  1179→
  1180→(Describe the initiative's background and goals)
  1181→
  1182→## References
  1183→
  1184→(List relevant files, docs, or prior work)
  1185→----
  1186→. Create new
  1187→{jjdhr_heat}
  1188→with:
  1189→.. Key: `₣` + encoded
  1190→{jjdt_firemark}
  1191→.. {jjdhm_silks}:
  1192→from argument
  1193→.. {jjdhm_created}:
  1194→from argument
  1195→.. {jjdhm_status}:
  1196→{jjdhe_racing}
  1197→.. {jjdhm_order}:
  1198→empty array
  1199→.. {jjdhm_seed}:
  1200→`AAA` (index 0)
  1201→.. {jjdhm_paddock}:
  1202→computed paddock path
  1203→.. `paces`: empty object
  1204→. Increment
  1205→{jjdgm_seed}
  1206→(base64 arithmetic with carry)
  1207→.
  1208→{jjdr_save}
  1209→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1210→to
  1211→{jjda_file}
  1212→. Output new
  1213→{jjdt_firemark}
  1214→to stdout
  1215→
  1216→[[jjdo_slate]]
  1217→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1218→==== {jjdo_slate}
  1219→
  1220→Add a new
  1221→{jjdpr_pace}
  1222→to a
  1223→{jjdhr_heat}.
  1224→The initial
  1225→{jjdkr_tack}
  1226→is created with state

  2331→[[jjdo_scout]]
  2332→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  2333→==== {jjdo_scout}
  2334→
  2335→Search across
  2336→{jjdhr_heat_s}
  2337→and
  2338→{jjdpr_pace_s}
  2339→using regex patterns.
  2340→
  2341→{jjds_arguments}
  2342→
  2343→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  2344→* {jjda_file}
  2345→
  2346→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2347→* pattern (positional) — regex pattern to search for (case insensitive)
  2348→
  2349→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  2350→* `--actionable` — limit results to
  2351→{jjdpe_rough}
  2352→or
  2353→{jjdpe_bridled}
  2354→paces only
  2355→
  2356→{jjds_stdout} Grouped text output:
  2357→----
  2358→₣XX heat-silks
  2359→  ₢XXXXX [state] pace-silks
  2360→    field: ...match excerpt...
  2361→₣YY another-heat
  2362→  ₢YYYYY [state] another-pace
  2363→    field: ...match excerpt...
  2364→----
  2365→
  2366→Where `field` is one of: `silks`, `spec`, `direction`, or `paddock`.
  2367→Match excerpt shows approximately 60 characters of context around the first match.

  2370→
  2371→{jjds_behavior}
  2372→
  2373→. Read
  2374→{jjdgr_gallops}
  2375→from file
  2376→. Build case-insensitive regex from pattern argument
  2377→. For each
  2378→{jjdhr_heat}
  2379→(all statuses: racing, stabled, retired):
  2380→.. For each
  2381→{jjdpr_pace}
  2382→in
  2383→{jjdhm_order}:
  2384→... If `--actionable` specified, skip paces not in
  2385→{jjdpe_rough}
  2386→or
  2387→{jjdpe_bridled}
  2388→state
  2389→... Search in order (first match wins):
  2390→.... {jjdkm_silks}
  2391→(from `tacks[0].silks`)
  2392→.... {jjdkm_text}
  2393→(spec from `tacks[0].text`)
  2394→.... {jjdkm_direction}
  2395→(from `tacks[0].direction` if present)
  2396→.... {jjdhm_paddock}
  2397→file content
  2398→... If match found:
  2399→.... If this is the first match in this
  2400→{jjdhr_heat},
  2401→print heat header
  2402→.... Print pace line with
  2403→{jjdt_coronet},
  2404→state, and
  2405→{jjdkm_silks}
  2406→.... Print match line with field name and context excerpt (60 chars around match)

  2409→{jjdo_scout}
  2410→does NOT search steeplechase/chalk descriptions.
  2411→Steeplechase entries are for session history reconstruction; searching them would conflate "what we planned" with "what we discussed."
  2412→The scout searches plan artifacts only:
  2413→{jjdkm_silks},
  2414→{jjdkm_text},
  2415→{jjdkm_direction},
  2416→and
  2417→{jjdhm_paddock}
  2418→content.

  2554→=== Steeplechase Operations
  2555→
  2556→The steeplechase is Job Jockey's session history, stored in git commits rather than the
  2557→{jjdgr_gallops}
  2558→JSON.
  2559→Commit messages follow patterns that
  2560→{jjdo_rein}
  2561→can parse to reconstruct session history.
  2562→
  2563→==== Commit Message Patterns
  2564→
  2565→All JJ commits use the format: `jjb:HALLMARK:IDENTITY:ACTION: message`
  2566→
  2567→* `jjb` — JJ brand prefix (literal)
  2568→* `HALLMARK` — Version identifier. Format: NNNN (installed) or NNNN-xxxxxxx (dev). Source: `.vvk/vvbf_brand.json` (installed) or max hallmark from `Tools/vok/vov_veiled/vovr_registry.json` + git HEAD (Kit Forge)
  2569→* `IDENTITY` — ₢CORONET for pace-level, ₣FIREMARK for heat-level
